hard.next_step: 6/22 - No material update Steve Ovadje: Send Zycus materials focused on Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, and Merlin Intake. Prashant: Follow up with prospects for a pre-demo call in June.
hard.ais_why: Evan Bartholio joined as the Business Systems Engineer driving operations, supply chain, and AI initiatives with an explicit AI mandate; team is tired of just looking at reports and wants automation and AI to do the heavy lifting; Merlin Intake, autonomous negotiation, and supplier scorecards named as areas of interest.
hard.competitor: Status Quo / Do Nothing (Infor LN ERP + point solutions + an existing RFx/quoting system)

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold at the starting line: one high-level overview call on 04 May, then 63 days of silence, and the 22 Jun next step logged 'No material update'. No economic buyer, no champion, and only a single mid-level technical evaluator (Evan Bartholio) engaged. The 24 Sep close is not credible with zero commercial progress and internal discovery never having started.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr, Qualified 05 May, 62 days in Qualified stage as of 06 Jul with no stage progression. Last buyer touch 04-05 May (63 days ago); rep-side next step 22 Jun logged 'No material update', no buyer reply. Close 24 Sep (80 days out) would require moving through Formal Evaluation, Shortlist, Vendor Selected, Negotiation and Contract in under 12 weeks against a typical 12-15 month enterprise motion. The first intro call 01 May was a buyer no-show; only one real overview call (04 May) has occurred, and it was exploratory, not a demo. Cold beyond the 60-day threshold forces Off Track regardless of stage.
evidence: Next Step 22 Jun logged 'No material update' by Steve Ovadje: rep-side note, no buyer reply
Stage Qualified for 62 days, no progression
First intro call 01 May was a buyer no-show; only one overview call held 04 May, still exploratory
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or executive sponsor engaged; contact is limited to Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations)
Buyer framed it as 'exploration phase, just seeing what capabilities are out there' (Evan Bartholio, 04 May 2026)

metrics.narrative: The pain is real but entirely unquantified. On the 04 May overview call Evan Bartholio said Rocket Lab relies on its Infor LN ERP as the source of data for supplier risk and then does 'advanced analysis' and 'forecast some risk' manually, adding 'beyond historical data and current data we don't really have a good pulse on other factors' such as geopolitical risk. He summed up the appetite as 'tired of just looking at reports and looking at data, just let's have some automations and see if AI can do any of the heavy lifting.' No cost, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, spend-under-management, or savings figure has been named by anyone, and the pain owner (supply chain operations, Israel Powell) has not been through a discovery session. The move is to run a discovery call with Israel Powell and the procurement lead Evan referenced and quantify annual sourcing volume, RFx cycle time, and supplier-risk exposure to build a defensible ROI case.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. The only buyer-side people on record are Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager, an operations/AI-initiatives owner) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations); neither controls budget, and Evan positioned himself and his colleagues as 'a few steps removed' from the leadership who met Zycus at a conference (04 May). No OpportunityContactRole carries an Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor designation, and no pricing or budget conversation has taken place. What the evidence does show is that a procurement/supply-chain executive above Israel Powell would own a source-to-pay purchase decision, and government-contract (Govcloud) constraints suggest legal and IT will also need a budget seat. The move is to ask Evan directly who sponsors and funds a procurement-transformation project and to secure an introduction to that person.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evan Bartholio, Israel Powell; no EB role)

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria surfaced on 04 May are functional-fit signals, not a formal scorecard. Evan Bartholio named the modules that 'resonate the most' as sourcing (iSource), supplier risk and performance management, and Merlin Intake, and he twice emphasised the Microsoft Teams integration ('we're Teams... I think that could be really valuable'). He also raised a hard technical constraint: 'because of the contracts that we have through the US government we have Govcloud restrictions... what capabilities, if any, are limited?' The AI/autonomous-negotiation control model was probed in depth (whether ANA weighs more than price, human-in-the-loop). These are inferred criteria from an exploratory overview, not a confirmed evaluation checklist or BRD. The move is to convert them into a written requirements list at the discovery call before the technical demo.

decision_process.narrative: No decision or buying process has been articulated. Evan Bartholio described Rocket Lab as at 'an exploration phase, just seeing what capabilities are out there' and said his own next step was to 'gather the actual pain points and talk to the people who are living them' before scheduling a discovery call, indicating no formal evaluation, timeline, or approval chain exists yet. No RFI/RFP, no committee, and no procurement channel have been named. The call sequence itself shows a stalled process: a buyer no-show on 01 May, one overview on 04 May, then silence. The move is to co-design a simple evaluation path (discovery to demo to business-case review) with named dates and to identify who signs off.
sources: SF field history (no stage progression since 05 May)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown and, at Qualified stage, that is expected rather than a risk. No MSA, SOW, legal review, or procurement route has been discussed. The one live paper-adjacent thread is the Govcloud/US-government compliance question Evan raised on 04 May, to which Steve Ovadje committed a solutions-consultant follow-up ('I can confirm and follow up with you'); how Zycus deploys within Govcloud constraints will materially shape any future InfoSec and legal path. Evan also flagged he would need to 'loop in someone from legal' on contract management. The move is to close the Govcloud deployment question early, because it gates any later InfoSec and contracting work for an aerospace/defence buyer.
sources: Avoma overview call 04 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio, Steve Ovadje)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is articulated qualitatively by an evaluator, not owned or quantified by the person living it. Evan Bartholio described a fragmented stack: Infor LN ERP for transactional procurement, a separate system for supplier communications, and an existing RFx/quoting tool, with supplier risk handled by pulling ERP data and doing manual trend analysis with no visibility into geopolitical or external risk factors. Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) confirmed 'we are primarily using two systems... it's not concrete, one system or the other', which is the real operational pain, but Israel has not been through discovery. Evan himself said he needs to 'talk to the people who are living them' before a deeper session, meaning he is a mobiliser, not the pain owner. The move is to get Israel Powell and the procurement team into a discovery call to confirm and quantify the pain.
sources: Avoma overview call 04 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio, Israel Powell)

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Evan Bartholio is an engaged, AI-literate technical evaluator who ran a good discovery conversation on 04 May and named the modules of interest, but he has not advocated internally, has made no introductions to power, and holds no budget authority. Critically, he has gone silent for 63 days and has not replied to the materials follow-up or the 22 Jun outreach. He explicitly deferred ownership of pain ('talk to the people who are living them') and of legal/CLM ('would have to loop in someone from legal'). Israel Powell has been on only one call and shows no advocacy. Building a champion is the continuous priority here: re-engage Evan with a direct value ask, and if he stays unresponsive, multi-thread to Israel Powell and up to a procurement leader to find someone who will drive this internally.

competition.narrative: The only competitor is the status quo. Rocket Lab runs Infor LN ERP for transactional procurement plus a separate supplier-communications system and an existing RFx/quoting tool (Evan Bartholio and Israel Powell, 04 May); no named rival (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, Zip) has surfaced in any call, task, or field. Steve Ovadje mentioned Zycus 'competes with Conga' on CLM, but that was a Zycus talking point, not a buyer preference, so Conga is not a live competitor here. The real threat is inertia: Evan framed the effort as pure 'exploration' with no compelling event, so the deal can quietly die by no-decision. The wedge is to quantify the cost of the fragmented status quo (manual supplier-risk analysis, no external-risk visibility, fragmented sourcing) and position a unified Zycus platform plus Merlin AI as ROI-positive versus doing nothing.
sources: Avoma overview call 04 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio, Israel Powell)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum stalled after a single May overview call. The record shows setup activity (owner reassignment, stage and amount edits) on 05 May, then no buyer-facing progression; the 22 Jun next step explicitly reads 'No material update'.

competitive_position.summary: No named vendor is in the field; the single strongest current threat is do-nothing / status quo. Rocket Lab's incumbent setup is Infor LN ERP plus point solutions for supplier communications and RFx (Evan Bartholio and Israel Powell, 04 May 2026), and with the buyer only 'exploring' and no compelling event, the deal's likeliest failure mode is no-decision, not a loss to a rival.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal gone cold with strong AI appetite but no champion, no economic buyer, and status-quo inertia as the main threat; the win depends on restarting the conversation and getting into real discovery.
flags: Re-open the relationship now: last buyer touch was 04 May, so a personal, value-led re-engagement of Evan Bartholio is the single highest-leverage move before this dies by no-decision.
Break single-thread: reach Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations) and an above-him procurement leader directly, so the deal does not depend on one unresponsive technical evaluator.
Convert AI appetite into a business case: quantify manual supplier-risk effort and sourcing cycle time with the pain owners to make Zycus + Merlin ROI-positive versus doing nothing.
Close the Govcloud deployment question early: an aerospace/defence buyer's US-government compliance constraint gates any later InfoSec and legal path.

Zycus is well positioned on the agentic story; the gap is not positioning but engagement and a quantified case.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0, status Strong. The 04 May call corroborates it: an explicit AI mandate from Evan Bartholio ('leading some of the AI initiatives'), interest in autonomous negotiation, agentic supplier-risk monitoring, and Merlin Intake, and the stated desire to move off manual reporting to automation. Not over-rated; the appetite is real but not yet converted to momentum.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An AI-hungry technical evaluator went quiet 63 days after a single high-level overview call; with no economic buyer, no champion, and status-quo inertia, this deal is stuck at the starting line and the 24 Sep close is not credible.

win_position.text: Overall read: one exploratory overview call on 04 May followed by 63 days of silence, no economic buyer and no champion leave this at the very front of the funnel with no path to the 24 Sep close.

Genuine AI pull: Evan Bartholio, who leads Rocket Lab's supply-chain AI initiatives, said on 04 May they are 'tired of just looking at reports' and want AI to do the heavy lifting, naming iSource, supplier risk and Merlin Intake (AIS 8/10 Strong).
Single-threaded to a mid-level evaluator: Evan holds no budget authority and admitted he must 'loop in' legal and 'talk to the people living' the pain, neither of which has happened since 04 May.

The real threat is do-nothing inertia: Evan framed it as 'exploration phase, just seeing what capabilities are out there' on 04 May, with no quantified pain and no compelling event to force a decision.

deal_momentum.text: Engagement has gone cold: last buyer touch was 04 May and the 22 Jun next step logged 'No material update', a rep-side note with no buyer reply.
No progression past Qualified in over 60 days; the first intro call on 01 May was a buyer no-show and only one overview call has actually happened.
No customer-named dates, no request for the next meeting, and no commercial topics: momentum is stalled on every signal.

customer_commitment.text: Evan committed on 04 May to gather pain points and 'get something on the calendar, maybe late this week or next week', but nothing has been scheduled 63 days on.
No economic buyer or executive sponsor has appeared; access is limited to Evan Bartholio and Israel Powell, both operational managers.

deal_risk.text: Status-quo inertia is the primary risk: Rocket Lab runs Infor LN plus point solutions and is only 'exploring', with no compelling event to displace the current stack.
Govcloud restrictions from US-government contracts, flagged by Evan on 04 May, raise an unresolved compliance and deployment question that could constrain the deal.
Single-thread plus cold: if Evan stays unresponsive there is no second relationship to keep the deal alive.
